title: How to Use {{scriptName}}
subtitle: Step-by-step guide to run the downloaded shell script
step1_desc: Find gitsparse.sh in your downloads folder (usually ~/Downloads) and cd into that directory.
step2_desc: Grant execute permission to the script so your shell can run it.
step3_desc: Download files into the current directory. You will be prompted to confirm before downloading starts.
step3_note: The script will print how many files will be downloaded and ask for confirmation.
step4_desc: Use the -o flag to download into a specific folder. The folder will be created if it doesn't exist.
step5_desc: Use -y or --yes to bypass the interactive confirmation — useful in CI/CD pipelines or scripts.
step6_title: Access private repos or avoid rate limits
step6_desc: Set your GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) as an environment variable. The script reads it automatically — never hardcode tokens in scripts.
step6_note: 💡 Tip: Add the export to your ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc to persist it.
step7_desc: The Git Sparse script requires git ≥ 2.25. It will clone a blobless repo into the output directory and selectively checkout only your files.
step7_req2: The output directory becomes a git repo — you can use git pull later to update files.
step7_req3: If only part of a directory was selected, unselected files inside it are automatically removed after checkout.
